q: A book at rest on a table is an example of _______ equilibrium.
The chemical equilibrium is _______ in nature.
A dynamic equilibrium exists once a _______ reaction occurs.
The _______ reaction is a reaction where the reactants are converted into products.
The _______ reaction is a reaction where the products are converted back into the reactants.
If a reversible reaction is in a _______ system, the system can reach a state called a dynamic equilibrium.
In a dynamic equilibrium, the concentrations of reactants and products remains _______.
Chemical equilibrium is _______ in nature.
At dynamic equilibrium, the rates of forward and reverse reactions are _______.
A _______ reaction is a reaction that can proceed in both directions.
The reaction in which product cannot turn back into reactant is known as _______ reaction.
Closed system is a system that allows _______ to enter and exit, but matter can neither be gained nor lost.
The system of reaction in which no reactant leaves the reaction mixture is termed as _______ system.
The symbol of equilibrium constant is _______.
The effect of pressure has a significant effect on the _______ phase.
The effect of pressure depends on the total number of _______ of gases on each side of the chemical reactions.
If the pressure is increased, the position of equilibrium will shift towards the side with _______ moles of gas.
In chemical equilibrium, the change of pressure in both liquids and solids can be ignored because their volumes are independent of _______.
As pressure _______, the equilibrium moves toward the side of the reaction with more moles of gas.
Inside the sealed soda bottle, the two form of carbon dioxide are in _______ equilibrium.
The rate of the forward reaction is high and the rate of the reverse reaction is _______ initially.
As the reaction proceeds, the rate of the forward reaction _______ and the rate of the reverse reaction increases until both reactions occur at the same rate.
An equilibrium system that contains the reactants and the products are in _______ phase is a homogenous chemical equilibrium.
An equilibrium system that contains the reactants and the products are in more than one phase is a _______ chemical equilibrium.
If the external factors are altered, the system will shift to a _______ state of equilibrium.
The greater the stress, the _______ direction of the reaction shifts in response to the stress.
A catalyst does not affect the equilibrium _______ and there is no change in equilibrium.
The effect of _______ on chemical equilibrium depends upon the heat change (Delta H) of the reaction.
Pressure changes do not markedly affect the _______ phases.
Pressure has a significant effect on the _______ phase.
The effect of pressure depends on the total number of _______ of gases on each side of the chemical reactions.
According to law of mass action, the rate of the _______ reaction is directly proportional to the concentration of the reactant or product.
The equilibrium constant (Keq) can describe whether the reaction tends to have a _______ concentration of products or reactants at equilibrium.
The equilibrium constant (Keq) depends on _______.
If the Keq value is large (Keq >> 1), that means concentration of the products is _______.
If the Keq value is _______ than 1, the equilibrium lies to the right and there are more products than reactants.
If the Keq value is equal (Keq = 1), that means concentration of the reactants and products are _______.
Kc is the equilibrium constant with respect to the _______ of the reactants and the products.
Kp is the equilibrium constant with respect to the _______ of the reactants and the products.
There is no gas present and _______ is not applied.
The equilibrium depends only on the concentration of _______ in the system.
The Kc value is small (Kc << 1), the equilibrium lies to the _______.
The Kc value is large (Kc >> 1), the equilibrium lies to the _______.
The reaction proceeding to the right is called a _______ reaction.
The reversible reaction is carried out in a _______ to achieve dynamic equilibrium.
The composition of equilibrium mixture does not change by the _______.
At equilibrium, the rate of forward and _______ reactions become equal.
A _______ reaction is a reaction that can proceed in both directions.
A decrease in temperature would favour the _______ reaction.
Due to the high concentration of reactants, the rate of the forward reaction is _______.
